title: 10 Best Active Constituents of Crataegus oxyacantha (Hawthorn)

constituents.constituent_desc: The oleanolic acid contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a pentacyclic triterpenoid with various medicinal properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective effects. This compound helps relieve health conditions such as heart failure, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol levels. Oleanolic acid is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the hawthorn plant. Improper use of Crataegus oxyacantha's oleanolic acid can lead to some side effects, such as stomach upset, headache, and dizziness.

The ursolic acid contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a pentacyclic triterpene acid that is widely distributed in plants and is known for its various medicinal properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer activities, as well as the ability to improve muscle strength and endurance. This constituent has been shown to help relieve various health conditions such as heart disease, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and digestive disorders. Ursolic acid is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Some possible side effects of Crataegus oxyacantha's ursolic acid if used improperly include digestive issues such as nausea, stomach upset, and diarrhea. Additionally, it can interact with certain medications, such as blood pressure medications, and may lower blood pressure to dangerous levels. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using ursolic acid supplements.

The flavonoids contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha are a type of antioxidant that provide various health benefits. They have been found to possess cardioprotective, vasodilatory, and negative chronotropic properties, which can help improve overall heart health and function. Hawthorn flavonoids may help relieve health conditions such as congestive heart failure, high blood pressure, and irregular heartbeat. These flavonoids are primarily found in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the hawthorn plant. Improper use of hawthorn flavonoids can lead to some side effects, including nausea, dizziness, and headaches. Additionally, hawthorn flavonoids may interact with certain medications, such as beta-blockers and calcium channel blockers, so it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using hawthorn flavonoids as a supplement.

The hyperoside contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a flavonoid glycoside that has antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective properties. Its main medicinal properties include improving coronary circulation, reducing blood pressure, and preventing cardiac arrhythmias. This constituent is known to help relieve health conditions such as heart failure, atherosclerosis, and cardiac insufficiency. Hyperoside is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Improper use of Crataegus oxyacantha hyperoside can lead to potential side effects such as nausea, dizziness, and digestive issues.

The vitexin contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a type of flavonoid, a group of compounds with various beneficial properties. Its main medicinal properties include antioxidant, cardiotonic, and vasodilatory effects. This Crataegus oxyacantha constituent helps relieve various health conditions, such as heart failure, high blood pressure, and irregular heartbeat, by improving the function of the heart and blood vessels. Vitexin is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the hawthorn plant. Some possible side effects of Crataegus oxyacantha vitexin, if used improperly, include dizziness, headache, nausea, and digestive problems.

The swertisin contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a bioactive flavonoid that has been found to possess various medicinal properties. Its main medicinal properties include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective effects, making it a valuable constituent in traditional medicine. Swertisin has been shown to help relieve various health conditions, particularly those related to the cardiovascular system, such as hypertension, angina, and heart failure. This constituent is primarily found in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. However, if used improperly, swertisin can cause some side effects, including digestive issues, dizziness, and sweating. Therefore, it is important to consult with a healthcare professional before using any supplements containing swertisin.

The rutin contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a flavonoid glycoside that has antioxidant properties. Its main medicinal properties include improving blood circulation, reducing inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. This constituent has been found to help relieve health conditions such as high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and cardiovascular diseases. Rutin is mainly found in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Improper use of rutin may cause side effects such as stomach upset, headache, and dizziness.

The fisetin contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a flavonoid polyphenolic compound that is known for its potent antioxidant properties. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer effects, as well as the ability to improve cognitive function and cardiovascular health. Fisetin has been shown to help relieve various health conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, and neurodegenerative disorders. This compound is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Some possible side effects of fisetin, if used improperly, include stomach upset, dizziness, and skin rash. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking fisetin or any other supplement, especially for those with medical conditions or taking medications.

The quercetin contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a type of flavonoid, a group of plant compounds with various beneficial health effects. Its main medicinal properties include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardioprotective effects. This active constituent helps relieve various health conditions such as heart failure, high blood pressure, and high cholesterol levels. Quercetin is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Some possible side effects of quercetin, if used improperly, include headaches, upset stomach, and allergic reactions.

The kaempferol contained in Crataegus Oxyacantha is a type of flavonoid, a group of antioxidants that are believed to provide various health benefits. Its main medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardioprotective effects. Kaempferol has been found to help relieve various health conditions such as cardiovascular diseases, neurodegenerative diseases, and certain types of cancer. This constituent is found mainly in the leaves, flowers, and berries of the Crataegus oxyacantha plant. Some possible side effects of kaempferol if used improperly include stomach upset, dizziness, and skin rash. It is important to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any supplements containing kaempferol.

intro: Crataegus oxyacantha, also known as Hawthorn, is a medicinal plant that contains several active constituents with well-known therapeutic effects. The most important of these constituents include flavonoids, such as rutin, hyperoside, and vitexin, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by dilating coronary blood vessels, improving blood flow, and protecting against oxidative stress. Additionally, Hawthorn contains oligomeric procyanidins, which have been found to have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, increasing its contractility and pumping efficiency. The plant also contains phenolic acids, such as chlorogenic and neochlorogenic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Finally, Hawthorn contains triterpene acids, such asursolic and oleanolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects. Overall, the active constituents of Crataegus oxyacantha make it a valuable medicinal plant with a wide range of therapeutic applications.
